evidence: The PDF source shows a compact four-dot ellipsis (....) in the statutory text (page 4 of PDF), following the ND convention for ellipses per Bluebook and court style. The DB incorrectly converted this to spaced format (. . . .). Rule 11 specifies: 'ELLIPSES: ND opinions print the compact four-dot form "...."; if the DB shows a spaced ". . . ." but the PDF shows "....", that spacing is an ingestion artifact — propose the compact form.' This is that exact case.
note: This is the only substantive defect in the opinion. All other text matches the PDF source perfectly. The curly apostrophes in the PDF have been intentionally converted to straight apostrophes per DB policy (Rule 9), which is correct and not a defect.
